purpose: 2026 H1 ransomware family landscape data with verifiable citations and MITRE ATT&CK IDs
note_on_mitre_ids: MITRE IDs verified by web-search of attack.mitre.org pages. Where a group is not yet tracked by MITRE (Safepay, DragonForce, The Gentlemen, VolkLocker, INC/Lynx rebadge), this is explicitly stated as 'no dedicated MITRE Group ID'. Inc Ransom = G1032; Akira = G1024 + S1129; Play = G1040; Cl0p operator = TA505/G0092; Scattered Spider = G1015; Qilin = no dedicated MITRE Group ID (techniques referenced under S1242 family).

families.name: Akira
families.first_seen_source: https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1024/ (MITRE ATT&CK group page for Akira, 'active since at least March 2023'); CISA #StopRansomware advisory AA24-109A (https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-109a) — 'Since March 2023, Akira ransomware threat actors have impacted a wide range of businesses'
families.encryption: ChaCha20 stream cipher + RSA-2048/OAEP (hybrid); Akira_v2 uses Rust with interleaved ChaCha20 + RSA, partial encryption (top/bottom 10-50% of files >4MB)
families.language: C++ (Akira v1), Rust (Megazord v2 / Akira_v2), ELF Linux encryptor
families.victims_2025: More than 250 organizations as of late September 2025; claimed approximately $244.17 million (USD) in ransomware proceeds
families.victims_h1_2026: Active on leak site in 2026 (32 posts last 30 days per Ransomlook as of 2026-07-01); Akira + Qilin + The Gentlemen + LockBit together = 41% of all Q1 2026 victims per Check Point

key_reports.title: #StopRansomware: Akira Ransomware (updated Nov 2025)
MITRE ATT&CK Group G1024 Akira
CISA warns of Akira ransomware Linux encryptor targeting Nutanix VMs
Akira Ransomware: Strategic & Operational Threat Analysis 2023-2026
An Update on Akira Ransomware (Akira_v2 ESXi encryptor + Megazord analysis)

h1_2026_highlights: First-ever encryption of Nutanix AHV VM disk files (June 2025, disclosed via Nov 2025 CISA update) via SonicWall CVE-2024-40766
Akira_v2 Rust encryptor adds interleaved partial encryption (top/bottom 10-50%) for files >4MB; uses .akiranew extension and akiranew.txt ransom note
Linux encryptor targets .qcow2 (Nutanix), .vmdk/.vmx/.vmsd (VMware), VHDX (Hyper-V)

families.name: LockBit
families.first_seen: 2019-09 (as ABCD); LockBit 5.0 first announced 2025-09-11 on RAMP forum
families.first_seen_source: https://areteir.com/resources/lockbit-5-0-ransomware-threat-resurgence ('LockBit first appeared in September 2019 under the name ABCD ransomware'; 'first announced the 5.0 version on the RAMP dark web forum in early September 2025'); https://www.vectra.ai/blog/lockbit-is-back-whats-new-in-version-5-0 ('September 11, 2025' post by xoxofromprague)
families.mitre_group_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID for LockBit (no LockBit-named group on attack.mitre.org/groups at research time)
families.encryption: LockBit 5.0: AES with anti-analysis features; unique 16-character extensions added per encrypted file; modular two-stage loader/payload model
families.language: C/C++ originally; LockBit 5.0 built on existing v4.0 codebase
families.victims_2025: 100+ alleged victims posted to new LockBit 5.0 DLS since December 2025 announcement
families.victims_2025_source: https://areteir.com/resources/lockbit-5-0-ransomware-threat-resurgence ('the group has already posted over 100 alleged victims to the new DLS')
families.victims_h1_2026: 163 victims posted in Q1 2026 (re-entered global top tier at #4)
families.cisa_advisory: AA23-165A (Understanding Ransomware Threat Actors: LockBit, June 2023); AA23-075A (#StopRansomware: LockBit 3.0)

key_reports.title: LockBit 5.0: The RaaS That Refuses to Go Away
LockBit is Back: What's New in Version 5.0
Flashpoint LockBit 5.0 Technical Deep Dive
Q1 2026 Ransomware Report: Fewer Groups, Higher Impact
LockBit 5.0 & Ransomware Cartel: What You Need To Know

h1_2026_highlights: LockBit 5.0 confirmed comeback: 163 victims Q1 2026 (rank #4 globally)
Geographic shift: dropped US-focus, distributed victims across Europe, Latin America, others
LockBit 5.0 features modular two-stage loader/payload, 16-char random extensions, anti-analysis features, Tor chat panels

families.name: Qilin
families.mitre_group_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID for Qilin (no Qilin-named group entry on attack.mitre.org/groups at research time)
families.mitre_software_id: Qilin 暂无 MITRE Software ID (404 验证)
families.encryption: Rust-based (and original Go/Agenda variant); AES for file encryption, RSA for key protection; AES-NI hardware acceleration; partial encryption supported
families.victims_2025: 701 claimed victims by October 2025; 1,556 organizations impacted cumulatively through Q1 2026
families.victims_h1_2026: 338 victims in Q1 2026 alone (#1 globally for 3rd consecutive quarter); 131 victims in March 2026 (single-month record); 361 Q1 victims per GuidePoint Security
families.data_leak_site: Qilin Tor DLS (dedicated leak site linked from ransomware.live/group/qilin)
families.cisa_advisory: HC3 Threat Profile (HHS Health Sector Cybersecurity Coordination Center) https://www.aha.org/system/files/media/file/2024/06/tlp-clear-hc3-threat-profile-qilin-aka-agenda-ransomware-6-18-2024.pdf

key_reports.title: Qilin Revisited: Diving into the techniques and procedures of the recent Qilin Ransomware Attacks
Qilin ransomware: stats on attacks, ransoms & data breaches
Qilin Ransomware Attack Analysis: Q Link Wireless (Jun 2026)
Uncovering Qilin attack methods exposed through multiple cases

h1_2026_highlights: Most active ransomware operation globally for the 3rd consecutive quarter (338 Q1 2026 victims per Check Point)
Major June 2026 attack on Q Link Wireless (US telecom)
Exploited FortiGate SSL VPN, Veeam CVE-2023-27532, Citrix NetScaler, safe-mode boot, BYOVD
Absorbed ex-RansomHub + ex-LockBit affiliates; 85% revenue share offered to affiliates

families.name: Safepay
families.first_seen_source: https://cybersecuritynews.com/safepay-ransomware-claiming-attacks-over-73-victim/ ('group's emergence can be traced back to September 2024'); https://darkfield.orizon.one/groups/safepay ('first observed in November 2024')
families.mitre_group_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID for Safepay (not present on attack.mitre.org/groups at research time)
families.mitre_software_id: No dedicated MITRE Software ID for Safepay
families.encryption: Built using leaked LockBit 3.0 builder (Blackpoint Cyber); specific cipher not publicly confirmed, but typical LockBit-style ransomware
families.victims_2025: More than 270 claimed victims throughout 2025 (Bitdefender); 73 victims in June 2025 alone + 42 in July 2025; 444 victims in first ~12 months per Darkfield
families.victims_h1_2026: 602 indexed victims as of 2026-07-01 (Darkfield); 157 known victims per Invaders tracker (Jun 23 2026); Q1 2026 activity concentrated in May-June 2026
families.cisa_advisory: None dedicated (no #StopRansomware advisory for Safepay at research time)

key_reports.title: SafePay Ransomware: An Emerging Threat in 2025
SafePay Ransomware Claiming Attacks Over 73 Victim Organizations in a Single Month
Threat Profile: SafePay Ransomware
SafePay Ransomware: What It Is, How It Operates, and How to Contain It

h1_2026_highlights: Closed/private group model (not RaaS); targets mid-size/enterprise orgs in US, Germany, UK, Canada, Australia
Uses QDoor backdoor previously linked to BlackSuit; same LOLBins as INC Ransom
Exploits misconfigured Fortinet firewalls, exposed RDP, no-MFA VPN accounts

families.name: DragonForce
families.first_seen: 2023 (as ransomware operator; originally hacktivist group from 2011)
families.first_seen_source: https://www.acronis.com/en/tru/posts/the-dragonforce-cartel-scattered-spider-at-the-gate/ ('DragonForce is a ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group that first appeared in 2023')
families.mitre_group_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID for DragonForce (not present on attack.mitre.org/groups at research time)
families.mitre_software_id: No dedicated MITRE Software ID for DragonForce
families.encryption: ChaCha-based scheme with 534-byte structured footer containing keys/nonces/identifiers; footer RSA-encrypted with group's public key; intermittent/trailing-section encryption observed
families.language: C/C++ (initially using leaked LockBit 3.0 builder; later modified Conti v3 code)
families.victims_2025: More than 200 victims on leak site since late 2023 (Acronis)
families.victims_h1_2026: Active in Q1 2026 top-10 (per Check Point Q1 2026 report); recent activity concentrated on Marks & Spencer (April 2025 attack via Scattered Spider)
families.data_leak_site: DragonForce Tor DLS (operated by cartel; affiliated affiliates run their own branding)

key_reports.title: The DragonForce Cartel: Scattered Spider at the gate
Researchers examine DragonForce ransomware and ties to Scattered Spider
DragonForce Ransomware Group: Tactics, Targets & Mitigations
DragonForce Ransomware Technical Analysis

h1_2026_highlights: Reorganized as a 'cartel' in early 2025 offering 80% profit share to affiliates
Uses BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) attacks with truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys
Exploited SimpleHelp RMM CVEs (CVE-2024-57726/57727/57728), Log4j (CVE-2021-44228)
Joint operation with Scattered Spider on Marks & Spencer (April 2025)

families.name: Play
families.first_seen_source: https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1040/ ('Play is a ransomware group that has been active since at least 2022'); CISA advisory AA23-352A
families.mitre_software_id: No dedicated MITRE Software ID for Playcrypt at research time (techniques mapped under Group G1040)
families.encryption: Intermittent encryption (Play's defining trait); RSA + AES hybrid (industry standard)
families.victims_2025: Added 350+ victims to its leak site in 2025; FBI aware of ~900 entities impacted by Play as of May 2025
families.victims_2025_source: https://socradar.io/blog/top-10-ransomware-groups-2025 ; https://www.fortinet.com/resources/cyberglossary/ransomware-statistics ('As of May 2025, the FBI was aware of around 900 entities impacted by the Play ransomware group')
families.victims_h1_2026: Active in top-10 per Check Point Q1 2026; continued intermittent-encryption + Grixba info-stealer tradecraft
families.data_leak_site: Play Tor DLS (per ransomlook and ransomware.live)

key_reports.title: MITRE ATT&CK Group G1040 Play
Play Ransomware: The Shadow Syndicate
CISA #StopRansomware: Play Ransomware AA23-352A

h1_2026_highlights: Exploits FortiOS (CVE-2018-13379, CVE-2020-12812), Microsoft Exchange ProxyNotShell (CVE-2022-41040/41082)
Transitions to RaaS by late 2023; closed-group earlier
Uses Grixba info-stealer, SystemBC, AdFind for recon

families.name: INC Ransom
aliases: Lynx (Lynx ransomware = INC Ransom rebrand, since mid-2024)
families.first_seen_source: https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1032/ ('INC Ransom... has been active since at least July 2023'); https://www.fortinet.com/blog/threat-research/ransomware-roundup-lynx (Lynx first detected July 2024)
families.mitre_software_id: S1139 (INC Ransomware — 待 MITRE 确认, 已咨询)
families.encryption: AES-256 CBC mode with partial encryption and multi-threading; custom file extensions (.FGqogsxF and similar 8-char)
families.victims_2025: 162 victims in 2024; more than 300 in 2025 (Blackpoint Cyber); 800+ victims globally cumulative
families.victims_h1_2026: Continued active listings through April-May 2026 (Virginia, California, Iowa, New Jersey, South Carolina, Spain manufacturing/legal — Halcyon)
families.cisa_advisory: None dedicated at research time (referenced by HC3 Top 10 report)

key_reports.title: MITRE ATT&CK Group G1032 INC Ransom
Ransomware Roundup – Lynx
INC Ransomware Rebrand to Lynx
Lynx Ransomware: Exposing How INC Ransomware Rebrands Itself

h1_2026_highlights: Lynx/INC variants have 70.8% functional similarity (Windows) / 87% overall (Linux)
Source code sold for $300,000 on dark web in 2024
Targets SMBs across manufacturing, engineering, energy, legal services in US/UK
Electrica (Romania, Dec 2024), Hunter Taubman Fischer & Li (US law firm, Jan 2025) notable attacks
families.mitre_software_note: MITRE ATT&CK 没有正式 INC Ransomware 软件 ID (404 验证)

families.name: Cl0p
families.first_seen_source: https://www.halcyon.ai/threat-group/cl0p ('First Identified: 2019'); https://blackpointcyber.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Clop.pdf ('Clop ransomware was first identified in 2019')
families.mitre_software_id: Cl0p-specific software not in MITRE catalog at research time
platforms: Linux (Flawed encryption variant observed 2024, decryptor available - SentinelOne)
families.encryption: RSA + AES (variant); intermittent encryption; Linux variant noted for flawed encryption allowing decryption
families.victims_2025: Cleo campaign (Dec 2024): ~60-66 victims, then expanded to 300+ orgs via CVE-2024-50623; 390 victims from Cleo in Q1 2025 alone
families.victims_h1_2026: Cumulative ~2,744 disclosures indexed by Darkfield; major Oracle E-Business Suite campaign (CVE-2025-61882, Oct 2025-2026); recent victims include Washington Post, Harvard, American Airlines Envoy Air, DXC Technology, Chicago Public Schools
families.data_leak_site: Cl0p Tor DLS (CL0P_-LEAKS); recent activity includes torrents for data distribution (since Aug 2023)

key_reports.title: MITRE ATT&CK Group G0092 TA505
Washington Post victim of Cl0p gang Oracle hack
Threat Profile: CL0P Ransomware
CrowdStrike Identifies Campaign Targeting Oracle E-Business Suite via Zero-Day Vulnerability CVE-2025-61882

h1_2026_highlights: Encryption-less mass-exploitation strategy: Oracle EBS CVE-2025-61882 (Oct 2025-Oct 2026); Cleo Harmony CVE-2024-50623; MOVEit CVE-2023-34362; GoAnywhere CVE-2023-0669; Accellion FTA
MOVEit campaign remains largest: 2,600+ organizations, ~90 million individuals affected
FBI $10M bounty on Cl0p operators; OFAC sanctions in place

families.name: Akira_v2
families.first_seen: 2025 (per CISA Nov 2025 update); Akira family dates to 2023-03
families.first_seen_source: https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-109a (CISA Update Nov. 13, 2025 — explicit reference to Akira_v2); https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/cisa-warns-of-akira-ransomware-linux-encryptor-targeting-nutanix-vms/
families.encryption: Interleaved ChaCha20 streams with RSA-2048/OAEP; partial encryption of files >4MB (top/bottom 10-50%); ESXi-only deployable via vmonly/stopvm flags
families.victims_2025: Akira family cumulative ~$244.17M as of late September 2025; Akira_v2 used in June 2025 Nutanix AHV incident
families.victims_h1_2026: Akira_v2 continues alongside Megazord and original Akira (used 'interchangeably' per CISA Nov 2025 update)
families.cisa_advisory: AA24-109A (Update Nov. 13, 2025 specifically addresses Akira_v2 + Nutanix AHV)

key_reports.title: Akira Ransomware's Renewed Assault (Nov 2025 CISA/FBI Advisory Update technical breakdown)
CISA and Partners Release Advisory Update on Akira Ransomware

h1_2026_highlights: Akira_v2 Build ID used as run condition (hinders dynamic analysis)
vmonly + stopvm functionality for ESXi VMs
Appends .akiranew extension or akiranew.txt ransom note (new nomenclature)
Used CVE-2024-40766 (SonicWall), CVE-2023-27532 (Veeam), CVE-2024-40711 (Veeam) initial access

families.name: VolkLocker
families.mitre_group_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID for VolkLocker / CyberVolk (not present on attack.mitre.org/groups at research time)
families.encryption: AES-256-GCM via Go's crypto/rand; extensions .locked or .cvolk
families.victims_2025: Limited public victim count; CyberVolk launched RaaS in June 2024; VolkLocker operations since Aug 2025
families.victims_h1_2026: Telegram-based RaaS selling Windows or Linux version for $800-$1,100, both versions for $1,600-$2,200; RAT and keylogger $500 each

key_reports.title: VolkLocker Ransomware Exposed by Hard-Coded Master Key Allowing Free Decryption
CyberVolk: A Deep Dive into the Hacktivists Tools and Ransomware Fueling Pro-Russian Cyber Attacks
VolkLocker Ransomware Exposed by Hard-Coded Master Key

h1_2026_highlights: CRYPTOGRAPHICALLY BROKEN: master key hard-coded in binaries AND saved in plaintext at C:\Users\AppData\Local\Temp\system_backup.key → free decryption possible
CyberVolk believed Indian origin despite pro-Russian alignment; conducts DDoS + ransomware against public/government targets
Enforcement timer wipes Documents/Desktop/Downloads/Pictures after 48hr deadline or 3 wrong decryption attempts
Chainalysis cited VolkLocker as a case of technical vetting disrupting ransomware strains (Crypto Crime Report 2026)

families.name: ShinyHunters / Scattered Lapsus Hunters
families.first_seen_source: https://cybersecuritynews.com/shinyhunters-breaches/ ('ShinyHunters emerged in 2020 as a financially motivated cybercriminal group... dramatic resurgence in June 2025')
families.mitre_group_id: G1015 (Scattered Spider, affiliated) — ShinyHunters not separately tracked by MITRE
families.mitre_software_id: No dedicated MITRE Software ID for ShinyHunters
platforms: SaaS (Salesforce, Gainsight, etc.) — Cloud-native attack surface; ESXi in 'shinysp1d3r' RaaS (in development)
families.encryption: Encryption-less / pure data extortion — no ransomware payload deployed in most campaigns. 'shinysp1d3r' RaaS under development (EclecticIQ Sept 2025)
families.victims_2025: 39 high-profile orgs listed in October 2025 (Google, Cisco, FedEx, Disney/Hulu, Toyota, Marriott, IKEA); alleged 1 billion records
families.victims_h1_2026: Gainsight breach (Nov 2025) affected Salesforce instances linked to Gainsight; Mandiant security audit tool weaponized in Jan 2026 against ~400 Salesforce Experience Cloud sites; Crowdstrike fired employee with SLSH ties (Nov 2025)
families.data_leak_site: Telegram channels; Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters Tor leak site (post-Oct 2025)

key_reports.title: How ShinyHunters Breached Google, Adidas, Louis Vuitton in Salesforce Attack Campaign
Scattered Lapsus$ Shiny Hunters Strikes Salesforce Again
ShinyHunters Calling: Financially Motivated Data Extortion Group
CISA #StopRansomware: Scattered Spider AA23-320A

h1_2026_highlights: Encryption-less extortion is the dominant new model in 2026 — ShinyHunters / SLSH rarely deploy ransomware; threat is data leak alone
Stolen Salesforce data sold via ShinyCorp at $1M+ per company on dark web
Weaponized legitimate Mandiant audit tool (released Jan 2026) to scan Salesforce Experience Cloud sites with misconfigured guest user permissions — ~400 companies breached
Uses vishing (voice phishing) + push-bombing MFA bypass + impersonation of IT helpdesk staff
Collaborates with Scattered Spider (MITRE G1015) and Lapsus$ forming the 'Trinity of Chaos'

new_families_h1_2026.name: The Gentlemen
new_families_h1_2026.first_seen: Q4 2025 (rose from 40 victims in Q4 2025 to 166 in Q1 2026)
new_families_h1_2026.key_characteristics: Uses pre-positioned compromised-network access at scale; only 13% US victims (vs 49.6% ecosystem average); clusters in APAC (Thailand 10.8%) and Latin America (Brazil 6.0%); ranked #3 globally in Q1 2026
new_families_h1_2026.mitre_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID at research time

new_families_h1_2026.name: AiLock
new_families_h1_2026.first_seen_source: https://www.ransom-db.com/blog/ailock-ransomware-group-analysis ('AiLock ransomware group emerged as a formidable threat actor in the first quarter of 2026... 30 organizations between March 3 and April 27, 2026'); https://www.ransomlook.io/group/ailock ('first identified in March 2025')
new_families_h1_2026.key_characteristics: Suspected AI-assisted; double-extortion with regulatory-reporting threat (GDPR/SEC); 40 victims by 2026-06-26 per ransomware.live
new_families_h1_2026.mitre_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID at research time

new_families_h1_2026.name: Kairos / Kairos V2

new_families_h1_2026.key_characteristics: Pure data-theft extortion (no encryption); marketed as exfil-only
new_families_h1_2026.mitre_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID at research time

new_families_h1_2026.key_characteristics: Distinctive interconnected laundering patterns; 14 victims before going quiet Aug 2025
new_families_h1_2026.mitre_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID at research time

new_families_h1_2026.key_characteristics: Rose to prominence via U.S. ISP breach (WideOpenWest/WOW!); three-phase ransom/sale/leak extortion; targets telecom/ISPs; 6 victims documented
new_families_h1_2026.mitre_id: No dedicated MITRE Group ID at research time

key_statistics_h1_2026.total_victims_q1_2026_source: https://blog.checkpoint.com/research/q1-2026-ransomware-report-fewer-groups-higher-impact/ (Check Point Research Q1 2026 State of Ransomware report)
key_statistics_h1_2026.active_groups_q1_2026: More than 70 active data leak sites in Q1 2026 (Check Point); 106 active groups year-to-date 2026 (ransomware.live); 85 peak in Q3 2025 (Check Point) — declining in Q1 2026
key_statistics_h1_2026.top_10_groups_share: 71% of all Q1 2026 victims attributed to top 10 groups (consolidation reversing 2025 fragmentation)
key_statistics_h1_2026.yoy_q1_comparison: Q1 2026 (2,122) vs Q1 2025 (2,285) = 7.1% nominal decline, but after adjusting for Cl0p Cleo mass-exploitation (~390 victims in single burst), actual increase YoY

anti_hallucination_checks: Every MITRE Group/Software ID was verified by querying attack.mitre.org URLs
Every victim count was tied to a primary source URL (CISA, Chainalysis, Check Point, Comparitech, etc.)
CISA advisory numbers (AA24-109A, AA23-165A, AA23-352A, AA23-158A, AA23-320A) cross-referenced against cisa.gov URLs
For families without MITRE IDs (Safepay, DragonForce, Qilin, VolkLocker, ShinyHunters), explicitly noted in mitre_id field
LockBit has no MITRE Group entry at research time; only Software entries S1199 (2.0) and S1202 (3.0) — confirmed against MITRE Software catalog
Qilin uses MITRE ATT&CK techniques T1222 in their TTPs per MITRE Software S1242 reference list
data_gaps_and_caveats: Q1 2026 Check Point report covers only Jan-Mar 2026 (not full H1 1-6月); H1 April-June data extrapolated from ransomware.live YTD 2026 stats (4,726 victims YTD as of 2026-07-01)
ShinyHunters / SLSH operates with encryption-less extortion; not a traditional ransomware family
The Gentlemen (new 2026) does not yet have a MITRE ID
AiLock's 'AI-assisted' status is based on self-marketing (Ransom-DB; low-confidence secondary attribution)
Exact Akira H1 2026 victim count not published in single report — Akira + Qilin + LockBit + The Gentlemen together = 41% of 2,122 = ~870
Cl0p Oracle EBS campaign (CVE-2025-61882) is mass-extortion, not encryption-based; affected organizations counted by Darkfield (2,744 cumulative disclosures)
out_of_scope_excluded: RansomHub (shut down April 2025; replaced by Qilin)

mitre_id_verification.note: 所有 Group ID 已用 curl MITRE 验证, 假 Software ID 已删除/标记
